content.text:
# Materials Required to Build a Coffee Darkness Meter

A coffee darkness meter measures the roast level/color of coffee beans or brewed coffee. Here are the materials you'll need:

## Electronic Components
- **Microcontroller** (Arduino Uno/Nano or ESP32)
- **Color/Light sensor** (TCS3200, TCS34725, or photodiode)
- **LED light source** (white LED for consistent illumination)
- **LCD or OLED display** (to show readings)
- **Resistors** (assorted, 220Ω–10kΩ)
- **Jumper wires** (male-to-male, male-to-female)
- **Breadboard** or **PCB** (for prototyping/final assembly)
- **Push buttons** (for calibration and operation)
- **Power supply** (battery pack or USB power, 5V/9V)

## Optical Components
- **Light-blocking enclosure** (to prevent ambient light interference)
- **Sample holder/chamber** (transparent glass or plastic cuvette)
- **Diffuser** (for even light distribution)

## Housing & Structure
- **Project enclosure box** (plastic or 3D-printed casing)
- **Mounting hardware** (screws, nuts, standoffs)

## Tools Required
- **Soldering iron and solder**
- **Wire strippers**
- **Screwdriver set**
- **Multimeter** (for testing connections)
- **Hot glue gun** (for securing components)

## Software/Programming
- **Computer with Arduino IDE** (or relevant programming software)
- **USB cable** (for uploading code)

## Optional/Calibration Items
- **Reference color standards** (known roast samples for calibration)
- **Heat-resistant material** (if measuring hot coffee)

---

Would you like me to provide a **circuit diagram**, **sample code**, or **step-by-step assembly instructions** for this project?
